scenario_scope: Coordinated intake of official provider contracts and primary systems work on exact prefix caching, prompt modules, cache-aware scheduling, disaggregated KV state, non-prefix fusion, semantic output caching, and cache-hit pricing.

cases.decision_rationale: Fast Generation already owns KV allocation, prefix-reuse controls, serving architecture, and latency semantics. Resource Economics and Context Transactions already own the two bounded handoffs.
promotion_blockers: no semantic-cache calibration or lifecycle closure
non_claims: does not establish faster or cheaper inference
does not promote any chapter core

cases.decision_rationale: A standalone chapter would split one serving route across generation mechanics, economic allocation, and context validity while duplicating three mature owner boundaries.
required_before_drafting: not applicable; use the three existing owners
evidence_transition_preconditions: a future distinct owner interface would require fresh adjudication
promotion_blockers: topic breadth alone is not a chapter boundary
non_claims: does not reserve a future cache chapter

validation_boundary: Records an existing-chapter integration and rejects a duplicate chapter. It does not validate provider prices, a cache implementation, any latency or cost result, semantic equivalence, privacy, invalidation, deletion, or transfer.
non_claims: does not authorize deployment or cross-tenant cache sharing
